title: Wire 'Simulate This' button in TopologyAwareFailureModes (currently console.log)
description: TopologyAwareFailureModes.tsx:508-510 — the 'Simulate This' button handler only does console.log. Wire it to handleSimulate(selectedFailure) or remove the button.
acceptanceCriteria: Button triggers cascade animation for selected failure mode

title: Show feedback when Raft Submit CMD is dropped (no leader exists)
description:
raft.ts:355-356 silently drops commands when no leader. DistributedModule.tsx:3674 calls it with no result check. User clicks Submit CMD, nothing happens.
Fix: return boolean from submitCommand, show toast or disable button when no leader.
acceptanceCriteria: submitCommand returns false when no leader
UI shows visible feedback when command dropped

title: Fix module-progress.ts — only tracks 6 of 11 distributed concepts
description: module-progress.ts:82-89 tracks: cap-theorem, consistent-hashing, raft-consensus, gossip-protocol, vector-clocks, crdt. Missing: two-phase-commit, saga-pattern, map-reduce, lamport-timestamps, paxos.
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 distributed concepts in the progress array
IDs match SIMULATIONS array in DistributedModule.tsx

title: Split DistributedModule.tsx (4904 lines) into focused files
description: 4904-line god file with 11 canvases, 3 panels, 1 hook with 45 state vars. Extract: hooks/useDistributedModule.ts, 11 canvas files, sidebar, properties, bottom panel. This unblocks every other UI task.

title: Replace inline canvases with orphaned accessible visualizers (or port their ARIA)
description: 887 lines of dead code at components/visualization/distributed/ (RaftVisualizer, ConsistentHashRingVisualizer, VectorClockDiagram) have proper ARIA roles, labels, framer-motion animations, and IBM colorblind-safe palette. The active inline canvases have zero accessibility. Either integrate the orphaned versions or port their ARIA. Then delete the dead code.

title: Replace 60+ hardcoded hex colors with design tokens
description: 10+ color maps (ROLE_COLORS, NODE_COLORS, MSG_COLORS, etc.) at DistributedModule.tsx:132-2664 plus inline SVG attributes. The Learn panel and orphaned visualizers correctly use CSS variables and IBM_COLORBLIND from @/lib/visualization/colors. Migrate everything to the shared system.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex in canvas components
All colors use CSS custom properties or shared palette

title: Cap event log at 500 entries (currently grows unbounded)
description: setEvents((prev) => [...prev, ...mapped]) at 8+ locations. Raft auto-play at 100ms = ~6000 events after 10 min. Bottom panel caps display at 100 but state array keeps growing. Fix: ring buffer or slice(-500).
acceptanceCriteria: No visible behavior change for normal sessions

title: Rewrite all content: problem-first hooks, WHY steps, real-world applications, summaries
description:
ALL 11 concepts score F or D on teaching quality. The root cause is one systemic issue: content is definition-first, steps say WHAT not WHY, no real-world context, no memorable summaries.

This is ONE content strategy task, not 11 separate rewrites. Apply this pattern to every concept:

1. HOOK: Problem statement, not definition. 'Five servers need to agree...' not 'Raft is a consensus algorithm...'
2. INTUITION: Analogy before formalism. 'Think of gossip spreading through a school' not 'Epidemic-style information dissemination'
3. WHY STEPS: Add reason field to events. 'Because it hasn't heard from a leader' not just 'election timeout'
4. REAL-WORLD: 'Used by etcd, CockroachDB, TiKV' per concept
5. SUMMARY: 3 bullet flashcard-ready takeaway
6. COMMON MISTAKES: 1-2 per concept (CAP 'pick 2' myth, Raft heartbeat confusion, etc.)

Sample rewrites provided in the audit for Raft, Consistent Hashing, Gossip, CAP, Paxos.
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 descriptions start with a problem, not a definition
At least 5 event types per engine include WHY in description
All 11 have 'Used By' real-world section in properties

title: Add pseudocode panels for all 11 simulations
description: Zero pseudocode anywhere. This is the single biggest gap for an educational platform. Write academic-style pseudocode (Raft paper Figure 2 style, not TypeScript) that maps to each engine. Add toggleable panel with current-line highlighting during step-through.

title: Add prediction mode: 'What happens next?' before each step
description:
The single most impactful practice feature. Testing effect research: retrieval practice = 50-70% better retention than passive review. Before key actions, prompt user to predict the outcome.

Examples:
- Raft: 'Which node will win the election? Click your prediction.'
- CAP: 'Will this write succeed in CP mode during partition?'
- CRDTs: 'After merge, what will A's value be?'
- 2PC: 'P2 voted NO. Commit or Abort?'
- Gossip: 'After this round, how many nodes have the data?'

Show correct/incorrect with brief WHY. User can disable predictions.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 prediction prompts per simulation

title: Surface existing interview questions from concepts-data.ts in module UI
description: concepts-data.ts has 4-5 interview questions per concept with model answers for CAP, Consistent Hashing, Gossip, Raft, 2PC, Saga — already written, just not shown in the distributed module. Surface as 'Interview Questions' section in properties panel. Expandable to reveal answers.
acceptanceCriteria: Questions expandable to reveal model answers

title: Add Consistency Models interactive simulation (CRITICAL curriculum gap)
description: The #1 missing topic. In MIT 6.5840 LEC 8, DDIA Ch 9, and top-5 interview topic. Build: same read/write operations shown under 4 models (Linearizable, Sequential, Causal, Eventual) with visual diff of outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: User toggles between models, sees SAME operations produce DIFFERENT results
Properties panel explains each model with real database examples

title: Add Replication simulation (SEO content exists, needs interactive sim)
description: concepts-data.ts (slug: 'replication') already has 3-paragraph explanation + 4 interview questions. Build: Leader-Follower (sync/async toggle showing lag), Multi-Leader (write conflicts), Leaderless (adjustable NWR quorum).
acceptanceCriteria: Quorum mode has N, W, R sliders

title: Add Sharding simulation (SEO content exists, needs interactive sim)
description: concepts-data.ts (slug: 'sharding') has rich content. Build: range-based (show hot spots), hash-based (show distribution), directory-based. Let user add/remove shards and see redistribution. Natural link to existing Consistent Hashing sim.
acceptanceCriteria: Hot spot visible in range-based mode
Cross-reference to Consistent Hashing in properties

title: Add Leader Election simulation (Bully, Ring, Lease-based)
description: Foundational to Raft/Paxos but never shown independently. MIT 6.5840, Cornell CS5414 cover it. Show how Raft's randomized timeout is a specific instance.
acceptanceCriteria: User can crash leader and watch re-election

title: Write tests for 4 untested engines + expand 4 minimal test files
description:
Lamport, Saga, MapReduce, CRDTs — zero dedicated tests. Gossip (28 lines), CAP (23), 2PC (24), Paxos (25) are smoke tests only.

Key tests needed:
- Lamport: receive rule max(local, received)+1
- Saga: compensation reverse order, fail-at-step-1 boundary
- MapReduce: empty input, special char stripping
- CRDTs: OR-Set concurrent add/remove, LWW tie-breaking, GCounter non-negative
- Gossip: convergence with killed node
- CAP: minority write rejection in CP mode
acceptanceCriteria: 4 new test files with 6+ cases each
4 expanded test files with 6+ cases each

title: Complete existing simulations: Gossip variants, Saga orchestration, PACELC, Multi-Paxos
description:
4 simulations have important missing variants that the SEO content already describes:
1. Gossip: only push implemented. SEO mentions push/pull/push-pull. Add toggle.
2. Saga: only choreography. SEO explains orchestration. Add mode.
3. CAP: SEO mentions PACELC (line 131,139) but not simulated. Add latency-consistency toggle for non-partition state.
4. Paxos: single-decree only. Multi-Paxos (stable leader optimization) needed for real-world relevance.

One task because these are all 'add variant X to simulation Y' — same pattern, same effort profile.
acceptanceCriteria: CAP has PACELC mode for non-partition state
Paxos has Multi-Paxos mode with leader optimization

title: Fix visual accuracy: Raft log truncation, hidden virtual nodes, missing legends
description:
Three visual lies in one task (same pattern: visualization hides information):
1. DistributedModule.tsx:387 — node.log.slice(-3) hides entries with no truncation indicator
2. ConsistentHashCanvas never renders virtual nodes despite engine having 50 per physical node — the core reason distribution works is invisible
3. Six canvases have no legend (Consistent Hash, Saga, Raft, CAP, 2PC, Paxos) — user must guess what colors mean

Fix all three: show full log (or '+N more'), add vnode toggle, add legends to all canvases.
acceptanceCriteria: Raft: all log entries visible or truncation indicated

title: Reorder sidebar + add difficulty tags and prerequisite indicators
description:
Current order is ad-hoc. Optimal learning path: CAP → Lamport → Vector Clocks → Consistent Hashing → Gossip → CRDTs → 2PC → Saga → Raft → Paxos → MapReduce.

Add difficulty badges (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced) and prerequisite hints ('Learn Lamport Timestamps first').
acceptanceCriteria: SIMULATIONS array reordered by learning prerequisite order

title: Connect existing gamification infrastructure to distributed module
description:
The platform has achievements, leaderboard, streak tracking, difficulty scaling, Socratic tutor, daily/weekly challenges — NONE connected to distributed module.

Minimum viable connection:
1. streak-protector.ts → daily practice streak
2. achievements.ts → 5 distributed-specific achievements
3. socratic-tutor.ts → 1 guided prompt per simulation
4. weekly-challenge.ts → 3 distributed scenarios
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 achievements for distributed module
At least 1 Socratic prompt per simulation
